Explanation: The report demonstrates strong clarity in presenting a structured three-step methodology for identifying abnormal chromatographic peaks in biopharmaceutical manufacturing. The writing is professional and well-organized, with clear section delineation and logical flow from problem statement through methodology to results. The depth of analysis is solid, with rigorous handling of data quality issues (60%+ missingness, 53,656 negative values) and appropriate statistical techniques (IQR-based outlier detection, proxy metrics using skewness and asymmetry). However, originality is moderate—the approach relies on standard statistical methods rather than novel techniques, though the application to summary statistics in lieu of time-series data is pragmatic. Scientific rigor is generally good, with systematic filtering and stratification by run/column. Limitations include: (1) visuals are present but somewhat limited in clarity and design (scatter plot colors are hard to interpret); (2) the conversion of skewness to "asymmetry" via absolute value is conceptually simplistic; (3) limited discussion of validation or sensitivity analysis; (4) appendix code has incomplete implementation (Code 3 references columns that may not exist in the dataset). The connection between identified clusters and actionable quality control recommendations could be strengthened with quantitative thresholds or follow-up studies.
